title: Somebody must see the sea
summary: A Norwegian lighthouse is now automatic. A computer does the work. But Mr Henrik still climbs the stairs every morning.
body:
Mr Henrik works at a lighthouse in Norway. The lighthouse stands on a small rock in the sea. It is white with a red top. Mr Henrik is sixty-five years old. He started this work forty years ago.

The lighthouse is now automatic. A computer turns on the light at night. A computer turns off the light in the morning. The computer does not need Mr Henrik. The government says, 'You can go home.' But Mr Henrik does not go home.

Every morning at six o'clock, Mr Henrik climbs the stairs of the lighthouse. There are one hundred and forty-two stairs. His knees are not young. He climbs slowly. He stops three times. Then he reaches the top.

At the top, he looks at the sea. He looks at the boats. He looks at the sky. He stays for thirty minutes. Then he climbs down. He sweeps the floor. He cleans the windows. He waters one small plant.

A young man from the city comes once a year. He asks, 'Mr Henrik, why do you still come? The computer does the work.'

Mr Henrik thinks. He says, 'The computer makes the light. It does not look at the sea. Somebody must look at the sea. The sea sees everybody. Somebody must see the sea.'

The young man writes that down. The young man goes home.

annotations.meaningVi: Na Uy (quốc gia ở Bắc Âu)

grammarNotes.title: Time prepositions: at, in, on
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Tiếng Anh dùng giới từ thời gian khác nhau cho từng loại mốc thời gian. 'at' đi với giờ cụ thể: 'at six o'clock', 'at night'. 'in' đi với phần trong ngày, tháng, năm: 'in the morning', 'in 2019'. 'on' đi với ngày trong tuần và ngày cụ thể: 'on Monday', 'on the 5th of June'. Bài này dùng nhiều 'at' và 'in'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Different time prepositions go with different time words. 'at' for specific times: 'at six o'clock', 'at night'. 'in' for parts of the day, months, years: 'in the morning', 'in 2019'. 'on' for days and dates: 'on Monday', 'on the 5th of June'. The passage uses 'at' and 'in' often.

translationVi:
Ông Henrik làm việc ở một ngọn hải đăng ở Na Uy. Ngọn hải đăng đứng trên một hòn đá nhỏ ngoài biển. Nó màu trắng, đỉnh màu đỏ. Ông Henrik sáu mươi lăm tuổi. Ông bắt đầu công việc này bốn mươi năm trước.

Giờ ngọn hải đăng tự động. Máy tính bật đèn vào ban đêm. Máy tính tắt đèn vào buổi sáng. Máy tính không cần ông Henrik. Chính phủ bảo: 'Ông có thể về nhà.' Nhưng ông Henrik không về.

Mỗi sáng lúc sáu giờ, ông Henrik leo cầu thang hải đăng. Có một trăm bốn mươi hai bậc. Đầu gối ông không còn trẻ. Ông leo chậm. Ông nghỉ ba lần. Rồi ông lên tới đỉnh.

Ở trên đỉnh, ông nhìn ra biển. Ông nhìn những con thuyền. Ông nhìn bầu trời. Ông ở đó ba mươi phút. Rồi ông leo xuống. Ông quét sàn. Ông lau cửa kính. Ông tưới một cây nhỏ.

Một anh trẻ từ thành phố đến mỗi năm một lần. Anh hỏi: 'Ông Henrik ơi, sao ông vẫn đến? Máy tính làm xong hết rồi mà.'

Ông Henrik nghĩ. Ông nói: 'Máy tính tạo ra ánh sáng. Nó không nhìn biển. Phải có người nhìn biển. Biển nhìn tất cả mọi người. Phải có người nhìn biển.'

Anh trẻ ghi câu đó lại. Rồi anh trẻ về nhà.

options: At a lighthouse on a small rock in the sea
questions.explanation: 'The lighthouse stands on a small rock in the sea.'

questions.prompt: How old is Mr Henrik, and how long has he been doing this work?
options: Forty years old, started this year
Sixty-five years old, started forty years ago
questions.explanation: 'Mr Henrik is sixty-five years old. He started this work forty years ago.'

questions.prompt: Who turns the light on and off now?
questions.explanation: 'A computer turns on the light at night. A computer turns off the light in the morning.'

questions.prompt: What does the government tell Mr Henrik?
How many stairs are there in the lighthouse?
questions.explanation: 'There are one hundred and forty-two stairs.'

questions.prompt: How does Mr Henrik climb the stairs?
options: Slowly, and he stops three times
questions.explanation: 'He climbs slowly. He stops three times.'

questions.prompt: What does Mr Henrik do at the top of the lighthouse?
options: He looks at the sea, the boats, and the sky for thirty minutes.
questions.explanation: 'He looks at the sea. He looks at the boats. He looks at the sky. He stays for thirty minutes.'

questions.prompt: How often does the young man from the city come?
questions.explanation: 'A young man from the city comes once a year.'

questions.prompt: Why does Mr Henrik say he still comes?
options: Because the government pays him a lot.
Because the computer makes the light, but it does not look at the sea — somebody must.
Because he cannot find a new job.
Because he is lonely at home.
questions.explanation: 'The computer makes the light. It does not look at the sea. Somebody must look at the sea.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A machine can do a job, but a person can still give it human attention.
Computers are bad and should not be used.
Old people should stop climbing stairs.
questions.explanation: Mr Henrik does not turn the light on or off; he keeps a kind of watch that the computer cannot.
questions.hint: Think about what Mr Henrik says the computer cannot do.

blanks.explanation: He climbs slowly because his knees are not young.
The computer does not look at the sea.

criticalThinkingQuestion: A machine can do Mr Henrik's job, but he says somebody still has to see the sea. Is there a task in your life that no machine could do not because the work is too hard, but because it needs to be witnessed? What is the difference between doing a thing and seeing a thing?
